plan_adherence_notes: The execute commit followed the additive plan: it preserved the pre-existing root service-index account-route assertion, exposure-route 405 assertion, never-traded exposure 404 assertion, and successful GET route-vs-getter parity coverage, then added the planned risk-only regression through an accepted probability-edit plus a confirming /risk 200. The only caveat is that successful GET dispatch is still proven by the existing parity test in the exposure-projection section rather than by a new dedicated routing-only test.
reason: The branch now covers the requested contracts across tests/test_bayes_market_api.py, and pytest -q tests/test_bayes_market_api.py passed with 225 tests green. The research and questions identified the real missing gap correctly and the implementation closed it without touching production code; only the GET-dispatch proof remains an interpretive reuse of the existing parity test instead of a new co-located routing assertion.
